mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals an easing of monetary policy, reducing discount rates and increasing present value of future cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary easing historically precedes equity rallies
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward return correlation with 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is not negative (i.e., <= 0) over any 2-year window, or mean return during falling FEDFUNDS periods does not exceed mean return during rising periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE above 4.5% historically precedes market downturns
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return when UNRATE > 4.5% is not negative, or t-test fails to reject null of zero mean at p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS rising) and stable monetary policy (FEDFUNDS unchanged or declining) indicates robust demand without tightening pressure, boosting equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS <= previous month value
failure_condition: Mean 20-day return when both conditions are met is not positive, or is not statistically larger than mean return when conditions are not met (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over 6 months signals strong manufacturing activity and supply expansion, which supports corporate earnings and lifts S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon, but this effect reverses when capacity constraints emerge.
failure_condition: If in the top quartile of INDPRO level (above 105), the 60-day forward return is negative on average, or if the positive effect does not hold in out-of-sample walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,400k units signals a slowdown in residential investment, which drags on GDP and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day window.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator
failure_condition: Mean 5-day return when HOUST < 1400 is not negative (t-test p <= 0.05), or less than 60% of triggered instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) signally a robust labor market that boosts consumption and profits, pushing S&P 500 higher over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200,000 AND UNRATE month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: Mean 20-day return in triggered periods is not positive, or is not significantly different from baseline (p > 0.05 in one-sided t-test).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth in the most recent quarter, adjusted for inflation, reflects overall economic momentum. Positive quarterly GDP growth persistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, independent of other signals.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 return is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or average return following negative GDP quarters is not lower than following positive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A spike in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.4%) signals overheating and prompts anticipated Fed tightening, raising discount rates and lowering equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation readings historically lead to short-term market drops
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following high CPI prints is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns does not exceed 55%.
